examples: An old Arab proverb says, "The enemy of my enemy is my friend."
The woman exclaimed, and thence the proverb: It is so mahogany!
A proverb is a short, well known saying that expresses a common truth or belief.
He even quoted an old Chinese proverb about building a "golden bridge" for your opponent.
More to the point, does it have no proverb expressing what the English one does?
